top_signals.title: Iraq to boost pipeline crude exports to 770,000 bpd
top_signals.summary: Iraq plans to increase crude oil exports through pipelines to 770,000 barrels per day from 220,000 bpd within two and a half months, according to a government statement. The expansion forms part of a broader strategy to diversify export routes and decrease dependence on Gulf shipping lanes. Iraq currently exports crude oil through the Turkish ...

top_signals.title: Steel cutting marks the start of the construction of the first Green Handy
top_signals.summary: The construction of the first methanol-powered Green Handy vessel has commenced on 1 June at China Merchants Jinling Shipyard in Nanjing, China, with a traditional steel-cutting ceremony. The milestone represents the transition from planning to production in ESL Shipping’s investment in a new generation of vessels designed for fossil-free fuels and improved operational performance. The ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Copper Tops $14,000 With Banks Calling for Even More Upside
top_signals.summary: Copper is trading just above $14,000 a ton in London, roughly $500 shy of its all-time high set in January, and Wall Street thinks it has further to run. Goldman Sachs lifted its end-2026 copper price target by more than 10% this week, raising its forecast to $13,735/ton from a previous $12,465/ton. The revision is driven by a weaker-than-expected supply outlook: the bank slashed its global mine supply estimate by 350,000 tons, citing ongoing operational disruptions at Indonesia's Grasberg complex and Ivanhoe Mines' Kamoa-Kakula operation in the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square Set to Exit Universal Music Stake
top_signals.summary: Bill Ackman is looking to sell his stake in Universal Music Group NV just days after the Amsterdam-listed group rejected a takeover bid by the hedge fund billionaire.

top_signals.summary: Lloyd’s Register (LR) has joined forces with HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering (KSOE) and HD Hyundai Heavy Industries (HHI) to develop a new generation of LNG carrier designs using hybrid electric propulsion. top_signals.title: Samsung Heavy wins $2.7 billion FLNG order for North American client
top_signals.summary: Samsung Heavy Industries has won an order worth 4.33 trillion won ($2.7 billion) to build a floating liquefied natural gas (FLNG) facility for a North American client, the shipbuilder said in a regulatory filing on Tuesday. top_signals.title: Chinese seaport cities see soaring port economy output in 2025
top_signals.summary: Chinese seaport cities saw the added value of their port economy hit 7 trillion yuan (about 1.03 trillion U.S. dollars) in 2025, according to a report released by an institute under the Ministry of Transport on Tuesday.
